result: The projection-loss mechanism persists. The n-weighted bilateral channel cancels; the positive orientation readout is nonzero; the coefficient-stripped channel is the weight-1/2 shadow; S/T multipliers are forced by the retained finite quadratic module.
boundary: The chi8 scalar character compression is not T-stable. The full retained residue carrier, or the 2D chi8/odd-indicator orbit, is required.
conjecture: For unary false-theta/mock-shadow channels carried by a finite quadratic module A with an even orientation coefficient vector a, the shadow map is the Phase Calculus projection-loss gate in the modular setting: the retained positive-orientation readout gives the false/mock side, and the symmetric scalar projection gives the lower-weight shadow/surviving modular channel.
falsifier: A unary false-theta/mock-shadow pair with even orientation coefficients and declared finite quadratic module whose known S/T multiplier cannot be recovered from the retained residue carrier T phase and S Gauss kernel, or whose shadow is not the coefficient-stripped symmetric projection of the same carried state.
